I think we need APP-C representatives to verify if this schema name is the same for both SDN-C and APP-C.
we should be picking this up from values.yaml no?
Are we sure these references to sdnc are correct for appc also?  I see these in a few other places as well in this file.

I share Roger's concern that the NFS share doesn't get us the redundancy we need.  I think we should go back to using persistent volumes.  I think this makes it easier to separate the applications from needing to care about the choice of underlying storage type (Cinder,Ceph,Fiber channel etc.).  That's an operator decision.
Is the intent to remove apps from one-click after they've been ported to pure helm?  I just ask out of curiosity, I'm OK with this.

APPC component is based on sdnc. Therefore it has similar files. However these files differ (at least in service name).
I am not aware of an option to use in appc chart resources from sdnc chart unless we make them somehow common, which I am not sure is the right thing to do.
This is part of the component responsibility, not OOM.

The password can be controlled by environment variable SDNC_DB_PASSWD, gamma is the default value. I do not think that need to externalize default value in configuration.
You are right, it is defferent credential than db root password, it is sdnctl user password.
Are you sure? I login with this password to APPC apidocs.
Let's leave it in hands of the component to remove it. I prefer to do only the completely necessary changes here.
Looks like a mistake. Will fix.

This is applicatively (even though in configuration) hardcoded password. If it will change, application will need to update the configuration. I think making this configurable here will provide no benefit. Similarly, all other parameters could be made configurable via values.yaml, which I wouldn't like to do. Let's leave it to applications.

Due to appc chart changes, if deployed from createAll.bash, it is not optimally defined. For example:
onap          appc-nfs-provisioner-2953601297-4dt0r
onap          onap-appc-appc-0
onap          onap-appc-appc-dgbuilder-3092772525-831ln
onap          onap-appc-appcdb-0
Notice the extra onap-
Also there are dependencies on helm repo available, "make all" etc.
Therefore I think we should create a separate, additional mechanism for deploying transformed charts, which is beyond this commit.
I rethink this. Will add back and change appc naming.

I noticed that when replicaCount != 1 there is a problem to access swagger:
HTTP ERROR 401
Problem accessing /apidoc/explorer/index.html. Reason:
    Unauthorized
It's a default admin password that the instance will have. It has been the default for a while now.

This may be problematic if the component will be deployed as something like "helm install pre-prod --name appc --namespace onap. Resulting hostname will be pre-prod-app-0, which will break this logic.
I though about something like: node=$(echo ${hm} | sed 's/-[0-9]$//g')
It has a problem though also: if there will be more than 10 replicas, it won't work.
We can document the fact that a dash shouldn't be used in the naming if ODL clustering will be used until we figure out a better solution here.
That is a good suggestion. I have enhanced it to not have any limitations.
